data.title: As a user, I can trigger re-sync with locking, confirmation, and metadata recording
data.description:
## Description

**What**: The re-sync command orchestrates the full pull-back workflow: validates preconditions, acquires file lock, calls pull (S2) then merge (S3), presents change summary for user confirmation, handles tag transitions, and records sync metadata.
**Why**: Provides the user-facing entry point for the pull-back workflow with safety guarantees (locking, confirmation, tag preconditions) that prevent data corruption and workflow state violations.
**Scope**:
- IN: Re-sync command, file lock (TTL 30 min, stale-lock cleanup), design:awaiting_review tag precondition check, change summary presentation, user confirmation prompt, --non-interactive flag for CI, read-modify-write tag swap (design:awaiting_review → design:approved), sync metadata recording as ticket comment
- OUT: URL/auth (S1), REST API retrieval (S2), merge algorithm (S3)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, the re-sync command validates that the ticket has a design:awaiting_review tag before proceeding; if absent, errors with a message explaining the precondition
  ← Satisfies: adversarial review finding on tag precondition
- When this story is complete, re-sync acquires a file lock with 30-minute TTL; stale locks (older than TTL) are cleaned up automatically; crash during merge does not leave a permanent lock
  ← Satisfies: "Re-sync acquires file lock (TTL-based, stale-lock cleanup)"
- When this story is complete, after pull + merge, a change summary is presented (components added/modified/removed counts) and the user must confirm before artifacts are written; --non-interactive flag auto-confirms for CI
  ← Satisfies: "presents change summary for user confirmation; supports --non-interactive flag"
- When this story is complete, after successful merge, design:awaiting_review is replaced with design:approved via read-modify-write preserving existing tags
  ← Satisfies: "replaces design:awaiting_review with design:approved"
- When this story is complete, sync metadata is recorded as a ticket comment: Figma file key, timestamp, components added/modified/removed counts, behavioral specs preserved count
  ← Satisfies: "records sync metadata on ticket"
- Unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Considerations

- [Reliability] File lock TTL + stale cleanup; crash must not leave permanent lock
- [Testing] Test lock contention, stale lock cleanup, --non-interactive mode

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.
